The main reason I reload my gift card is so that I can pre-order items and it works wonderfully. When you pre-order on Amazon with a credit card you are not charged until the item ships, which can lead to this back and forth waiting game that is flat out stressful. Instead, I reload my gift card with the amount total and take it out of there. This was the item is paid for and I don't have to worry about the money coming out of my bank account later, should I forget or the item ship earlier than intended. Seriously, if you pre-order anything on Amazon use the gift card feature, it will save you later.

That said, I did have an issue with my purchase... The balance reload page offers several amount options (50/100/200/500), or a field to enter a different amount. I chose the option for $500 because I knew I would be making some sizeable purchases soon. I expected the balance to be reflected in my account within minutes, and the email I got confirming my order told me that's what would happen ("Typically your balance is updated within 5 minutes of your order.")

A few minutes later, it wasn't there. An hour later, it still wasn't there. A few hours later, it still wasn't there.

I contacted Amazon and was told that because of the size of the reload, there was extra verification needed on their end. This was surprising, because the reload page said nothing about this, the confirmation email contradicted it, and the amount I chose was one of the predetermined options.

So if $500 requires extra verification, maybe tell people that when they order.

Ok so, I had a Mastercard gift card type sent by email that could only be used online or with wallet if Apple Pay was accepted (it did not work where Apple Pay WAS accepted once even). It also could only work if purchase did not exceed worth. I had $2 something left on said card and did not know how the bleep I could use it, then I read Amazon will add it to your Amazon balance, where refunds and bonus type moneys are sent. It was relatively easy to do, once I read over the directions for safety sake and figured it out, and there instantly the money transferred to the balance. I think it is used as a default, so I am not positive if it is put back in the balance if it is used and then purchase is returned, but it is my money, so I don’t think that’s an issue. I once got a $5 reward or something from Amazon and when I had to return the purchase, I was not given back that reward they gave me, so I am reticent when it comes to returns and Amazon balances.

Inside the box of the original order was a white envelope with Amazon`s logo . When I opened the envelope, there was a card with a Christmas image on the front and an empty blank white envelope. Living in Georgia, we rarely get snow, especially snow that sticks. When I saw I could order a snowflake from Amazon, I was ecstatic! I placed the order immediately, and received it the next day with free overnight shipping. That's where my excitement ended. I ran to the door to greet the UPS driver, and rushed to open the box. To my dismay, the 70° weather that day was my biggest foe. Upon opening the box, I was greeted with a puddle of water. I had planned on ordering 1,000,000 more of these immediately before Christmas so we could finally have a white Christmas here, but Mother Nature isn't having any of it. Perhaps this would be better suited for the northernmost part of the United States, but alas, down here in the hot/humid south, it's just not meant to be...

On the bright side though, I was able to redeem the code in the puddle for $50 worth of Amazon goods, so it wasn't all bad!

I don't know why more companies don't do this. I was first introduced to this concept by "Talbots" so many years ago. Worked out great for last minute and spontaneous gifts. In a pinch for time, I would write the amount on the card, take a photo of the back of the card to capture data, mail it out, then go online that evening to load the card with funds. On one occasion, I sent my elderly Mother a gift card with a marked value of several hundred dollars. I didn't want to worry about it getting lost or misplaced, so I wrote instructions to call me the day prior to use. Worked out perfectly, she actually called me from the store minutes before making her purchases. I went online, loaded the card with funds and Mom was able to use the card minutes later.

This is vastly more convenient and secure than buying a gift card at your local store. So safe that you could freely carry these around in your wallet or purse or vehicle for that matter, even with a preselected amount already written on the card (the key is to write the amount on the back of the card as well and take a photo of the back, then use your smartphone to add funds and activate AFTER you've given it out).
If you've ever hesitated on sending a gift card to someone because you were concerned that the card may not arrive or be delivered to the wrong address or recipient, this flex card would place your worries aside. If you're sending one of these cards and want to make sure it's received by the intended individual, you could enclose a note instructing the recipient to "call, text or email (your contact information here) to activate this card" OR upon removal of the Amazon activation sticker (on the front of card), you could replace it with your own custom label, instructing recipient on who they need to contact to activate card. You could even add a field on the label for your own security question to recipient, how's that for multiple layers of security.

And NOW, my personal wish. When will Amazon be offering gift cards that are charged to the senders credit card at the time of REDEMPTION. (I wonder how many gift card purchases are wasting away in drawers or misplaced across the globe). Of course this would have to come with some notification function (similiar to auto renewal charge of subscriptions) but that would be kind of nice, knowing that your gift had just been used (no other details necessary other than possibly name of recipient)
Amazon could be the leader in this "Pay when they Redeem" concept.

This is an awesome deal. I remember years ago I tried searching for Amazon gift cards on Amazon, and I couldn't find anything, other than gift "cards" that you could purchase, and then the recipient would receive an email only - no physical goods to speak of. And you know what? In my opinion, that sucks. Ya, there doesn't *need* to be a physical card, but when I give a gift, I'd like to be able to *give* that person something tangible. Enter: The Amazon.com Gift Card.

So, it's great that they offer a number of different designs at a number of different price points. One caveat: on the Amazon Mobile App on Android, it can be confusing as to how to choose the denomination and card design that you want. This is best purchased from the regular website. I got 3 different gift cards on here, each $20. Each arrived as promised. And why do I say these are a great value? Free 1 or 2 day shipping. AND, you get the gift card at no charge. In my opinion, the art on these cards are classy, and they're not printed on cheap paperstock either.

So, why didn't I give this 5 stars? I think Amazon could be a little clearer that adding a "Gift Note" to your order doesn't get the message printed on the greeting card. The greeting card arrives to you inside of a plastic mailing envelope, with the gift card mounted in the greeting card, and an envelope. The package is more suited for it to be delivered to *you*, and then you write your note, and then mail or hand it off to your recipient. I think I read some other reviews from people saying that they ordered these to deliver straight to their recipient, and the person received the aforementioned package. Oh, and if you order add a Gift Note during checkout, it prints on bottom of the packing slip... Not exactly ideal presentation, when there's a brand new greeting card and envelope in the same shipping sleeve...

[Update] I see they've since updated the description to mention the above "Gift Note" issue, and how exactly it is packaged.... Way to go, Amazon! This has been changed from 4 to 5 stars.

I purchased this gift card for a work-related holiday "gift swap."  I didn't know all of the people who were participating so I tried to think of a gift that everyone would appreciate. That wasn't a problem, because I found that Amazon would ship the gift card in a very nice gift enclosure card in only 2 days!!!  I don't think there is anything more convenient than that.  In this particular gift swap game, participants were allowed to "steal" co-workers' presents, and the Amazon gift card was stolen more often than anything else - it was definitely the most popular item at the party.  I would recommend this gift card to anyone, but especially the person who needs a gift and doesn't know the recipient's particular shopping preferences.  And, if you are a last minute shopper, Amazon.com is the place for you.  I will definitely purchase this gift card again.

I received this item today and boy, was I pleasantly surprised! What an elegant looking tin. It's a lovely presentation for a gift card. It's bigger than I thought it would be at "5-1/8" X "3/8". It's a stocking stuffer for my husband but I suspect we will probably hold onto it afterward and use it to give another gift card to someone next year. It's that nice.

That said, there are some things I should clarify, one being the free shipping. Amazon advertizes free one day shipping but when you place the order the confirmation says free two day shipping. This didn't matter to me. Hopefully, I am never in a position where it will. I ordered the afternoon of Dec. 17th, and it was at my door in 48 hours. Impressive. I am not complaining about getting a free gift tin and free expedient shipping, I simply want to point it out. It's somewhat confusing and deceptive.

The other point I wanted to make was about a message you can include with your order. Don't bother. It's simply a note written on the packing slip. If you need to address the gift or feel compelled to jot something down, might I suggest adhering a gift tag to the underside of the tin. Why do they have slots cut out of the bottom of the tin? I suspect it has something to do with being able to scan the card's bar code. Anyway, if you need to put a tag on it that's where I would do it.

I considered rating this item only 4 stars because of these two issues, but I truly feel that the item is still worthy of the high marks and the issues are too small to demerit.

Thank you, Amazon!

So while it sounds terrible to complain about a gift... I always hated it when people would give me those Visa or Discover gift cards. They are such a pain to manage and I've always had nothing but issues trying to use them. If you don' t have the full amount available on the card, some merchants won't let you use them. I would also be very careful about only using them for things that I knew I would never need to return... because what a nightmare that would be if you no longer had the card. Bottom line... I hate those things. But then a few months back, I had a revelation to simply use that Visa gift card to buy myself an Amazon Gift card (reload) and have it placed directly into my account. Regardless of the amount... whether it is some odd leftover like $23.78 or an even $100, you can load it to your Amazon gift card balance and dump those stupid Visa Gift cards. I know I'll definitely use the Amazon funds, and Amazons gift card balance management makes it incredibly convenient. Plus... some of those Visa and Discover gift cards lose value over time due to "maintenance fees." So if you're like me and hate dealing with those things, or throw them in a drawer and forget about them... do yourself a favor and convert them over to Amazon bucks. Life becomes so much easier!

I really don't know why this requires a review... It's a gift card. Well, here goes anyway -
They ship very fast and come in a nice little box that makes it easy to give to someone. The gift card is a magnificent flexible plastic with beautiful print on the front (design of your choosing). It looks and smells like plastic too! Not that fake plastic either. It's definitely real plastic. I had it sent to a lab to verify this. I found out that the plastic itself added some value to the card beyond what was loaded on it (fraction of a cent).
It's thin and can easily be put in your wallet or purse without taking up too much space. A real treasure to own.

Cons: Despite their very high quality, these don't last very long. Rarely do they last more than a couple of weeks before their value drops to zero. I've heard stories of folks who have made them last longer but they're usually people dedicated to the task with a lot of will power. This was not the case for me.

Alternate Uses: Don't be fooled by the description, these cards have many uses before, during, and after their value plummets to zero. Here are a few things that I have used them for...
- Applying Drywall Patch Material
- Smoothing out Caulk in corners
- Cracking open laptops for repair
- Getting into a locked room in my house
- All purpose, non abrasive scraper
- Paint guard
- Makeshift Ninja Throwing Star
- Small protective mat for gluing things

*Please note that Amazon sells products to do all these things already but they don't fit in your wallet. These things are like Swiss Army Knives, conveniently saving me time by being readily accessible. Like any knife, remember to clean it when you're done using it.
There is nothing NOT to like it. I highly recommend it to all other customers.

I have been utilizing Amazon on and off for a couple of years. I got started receiving a gift card from work. I have only placed one order using my credit card. Shortly after, I had fraudulent charges made on the same card. From that point on, I only use Amazon Gift Card to make purchases. I reload what I need when I want to make a purchase.

Instead of being in a envelope and/or attached to a thank you, congratulations card; it's a card within premium looking gift box.

Box itself is very sturdy, has a premium look and feel to it.

The cloth and card holder inside; not so much. Cloth is wrapped around a plastic card holder that's glued to the box.

Cloth is very thin, but functional for what's it's intended for. If you swap in-and-out cards multiple times, the cloth may rip; but for initial first impression it won't.

Box has a slide out design to it. Has a stop gap so doesn't slide all the way out unless you intentionally use more force than you have to.

Very nice finger ribbon to pull out the contents like a drawer

Back of the box has a space for name of person of sender and receiver.

Includes UPC and Serial No. number and bar code if you find an error with the order.

Gift card design is very basic; just red with amazon logos on it. Redeeming card is easy. There's a peel off sticker (instead of scratcher), so no need to worry about scratching off the text of the code. If you use the amazon app you can redeem the amount in seconds: click the camera option and align the boxes with the text code.

Doesn't show the amount on the card seeing it's customizable on the website. But it does give a pack in slip of paper to verify amount.

Aside from plastic card holder and cloth fabric being low quality; it still gives off a premium first impression

Gift card arrived activated, but would not apply to account. In support chat with Spotify, the problem was revealed to be associated with account bundles -- I previously had a student account that came with Hulu and Showtime. When my student account ran out (3 year max term), my access to these services terminated but they were still linked to my account, even though I had been paying the standard premium rate without Hulu/Showtime. The card could not be applied to my account with this bundle still linked to my account, support said. Spotify cancelled my premium and reverted the account to "Free." At that point the card applied to my account no problem (full 12 months). Do note that support confirmed that only 18 months may be active on your account, so if you try to redeem 2 of these cards, you will have to wait 6 months - 1 year to apply. Note these cards do not expire unless explicitly stated on the packaging (mine does not list an expiration date) - therefore these are not beholden to the "1 year expiration" for cash-value cards available for Spotify.
